title: rubus × ciliolentus
intro: Rubus × ciliolentus, a hybrid species of blackberry, is a type of bramble that is native to China and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, primarily for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of tannins and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rubus × ciliolentus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, teas, or extracts, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
